title: IREN Prices Upsized $2.6 Billion Convertible Notes Offering
text: IREN Limited announced the pricing of a $2.6 billion convertible notes offering on May 12, 2026. The initial offering size of $2 billion was increased, demonstrating strong investor demand. The convertible senior notes feature a 1.00% coupon and mature December 1, 2033. They carry a 32.5% conversion premium over the May 11 closing price of $55.15 per share, with an initial conversion price of approximately $73.07 per share. IREN stated that the offering size was increased from the previously announced offering size of $2 billion aggregate principal amount of notes. The company entered capped call transactions with a cap price of $110.30 per share, representing a 100% premium over the stock price at pricing. These transactions are designed to limit dilution from conversions. Net proceeds are estimated at approximately $2.57 billion. The company will allocate roughly $174.5 million toward capped call transaction costs, with remaining funds designated for general corporate purposes and working capital. The offering was scheduled to settle on May 14, 2026, with initial purchasers granted an option to buy an additional $400 million in notes within 13 days.
